agent_output: {"escalations":[{"type":"contradiction","description":"Bird declares courier assignment before IN_TRANSIT as a domain invariant — meaning the system must NEVER allow a delivery to enter IN_TRANSIT without an assigned courier. MJ's architecture explicitly violates this invariant by allowing IN_TRANSIT without a courier, deferring assignment to a background job within 30 seconds. These are mutually exclusive: either courier assignment is a hard prerequisite for IN_TRANSIT (Bird), or it is an eventual consistency concern handled post-transition (MJ). This cannot be papered over — it changes validation logic, state machine guards, error handling, and the customer-facing contract.","agents_involved":["Bird","MJ"],"routed_to":"Coach K","options":["Option A: Enforce Bird's invariant. Courier must be assigned before IN_TRANSIT. The state machine has a guard that rejects the transition if courier is null. Customer acknowledgement speed depends on courier assignment speed. MJ redesigns to assign courier synchronously or introduces an intermediate state (e.g., AWAITING_COURIER) between ACCEPTED and IN_TRANSIT.","Option B: Relax Bird's invariant. Bird redefines the rule to: 'A courier must be assigned to a delivery within N seconds of entering IN_TRANSIT. A delivery in IN_TRANSIT without a courier is temporarily valid but must not persist.' This makes it a soft constraint with a reconciliation window, not a hard invariant. Bird must explicitly approve this weakening.","Option C: Introduce an intermediate state. Add a state like COURIER_PENDING or DISPATCHING between order acceptance and IN_TRANSIT. Customer gets fast acknowledgement at this new state. IN_TRANSIT is only entered once courier is assigned, preserving Bird's invariant. Both agents must agree on the new state semantics."],"recommendation":"Option C is the strongest resolution — it satisfies Bird's invariant (IN_TRANSIT always has a courier) while preserving MJ's goal (fast customer acknowledgement). The new intermediate state makes the system's actual behavior explicit rather than hiding a temporary invariant violation behind eventual consistency. Route to Coach K to get Bird and MJ aligned before Shaq receives any implementation spec."}],"confidence":{"level":35,"high_confidence_areas":["The contradiction is real and unambiguous — one output says 'must never happen' and the other says 'will happen by design'"],"low_confidence_areas":["Which resolution Bird and MJ would prefer","Whether there are business constraints (e.g., SLAs, customer-facing promises) that favor speed over correctness or vice versa"],"assumptions":["Bird's use of 'invariant: true' means a hard, always-enforced domain rule with no grace period","MJ's 30-second background job window means the system will observably be in IN_TRANSIT with no courier for up to 30 seconds"]}}

content.thinking:
Clear contradiction between Bird and MJ. Bird says courier assignment is a prerequisite for IN_TRANSIT (invariant). MJ says IN_TRANSIT can happen without courier, relying on background job. These directly conflict.

Classification: contradiction. I must NOT produce a handoff brief. I must escalate to Coach K.
